subject: Continuous Random Variables
topic: §38 The Distribution of a Function of a Random Variable
question: The time, $T$, that a manufacturing system is out of operation has cumulative distribution function $F(t) = \begin{cases} 1 - \left(\frac{2}{t}\right)^2 & t > 2 \\ 0 & \text{otherwise} \end{cases}$. The resulting cost to the company is $Y = T^2$. Determine the density function of $Y$, for $y > 4$.
